description_long: This bungalow features a simple rectangular structure with a low-pitched, red hip roof and beige exterior walls that resemble wood paneling. The front facade is defined by a set of double glass doors flanked by two symmetrically placed windows, providing a balanced and inviting appearance. The side views reveal the full expanse of the roof, which extends slightly beyond the walls, offering some overhang for weather protection. The rear of the bungalow mirrors the front in wall material but has two windows without a central door, maintaining symmetry. The building sits low to the ground, typical of bungalow architecture, and lacks any visible porch or veranda. The overall design is modest and functional, suitable for residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story beige bungalow with a red hip roof, double front doors, and symmetrically placed windows. The design is simple, balanced, and lacks a porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing double glass doors and two symmetrically placed windows on either side.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the hip roof and plain beige wall with no windows or doors.
description_view_2: Rear view with two windows, no doors, and matching beige paneling and red roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to the other side, showing the roof's overhang and plain wall.
